question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract-call request with a crafted consensusTimestamp that causes load() to retrieve a stale ThrottleDefinitions file from the cache, resulting in throttle limits being evaluated against outdated quotas and allowing the attacker's transactions to bypass per-transaction-type gossip throttling?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: roundDownToHour(long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hour-granularity rounding in roundDownToHour() applied to feeSchedulesFileId and exchangeRateFileId to force the system to use a throttle/fee schedule from up to 59 minutes in the past, causing transactions that should be throttled under the current schedule to be gossiped without restriction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user, by triggering repeated contract calls that each produce a ParseException in codec.parse(), exhaust all 9 retries in the retryTemplate and force loadWithRetry() to return systemFile.genesisFile(), which contains genesis-era ThrottleDefinitions with permissive limits, thereby allowing the attacker to gossip transactions that would otherwise be throttled under current definitions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequence of requests that causes nanoSeconds.set(fileData.getConsensusTimestamp() - 1) to walk the timestamp backward through all historical ThrottleDefinitions versions until the genesis file is returned, effectively bypassing any throttle tightening that occurred after genesis and allowing gossip of otherwise-blocked transaction types?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a null-result cache entry (result != null check at line 134) to cause repeated cache misses for the throttleDefinitionFile, forcing repeated DB queries and creating a timing window where two concurrent requests both load different versions of ThrottleDefinitions, leading to inconsistent throttle enforcement and allowing some transactions to be gossiped past their quota?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger lazy initialization of the systemFiles map (via getSystemFiles()) under concurrent load, causing a race condition where two threads initialize the map simultaneously with different ThrottleDefinitions snapshots, resulting in one thread using permissive genesis throttles while the other uses current throttles, allowing gossip of transactions that should be blocked?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a consensusTimestamp of 0 or a very small value to load(), causing getFileAtTimestamp() to return no matching file_data row and triggering the orElse(systemFile.genesisFile()) fallback, which returns genesis ThrottleDefinitions with no per-transaction-type throttle buckets, allowing the attacker to gossip an unlimited number of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ArrayUtils.isNotEmpty(fileData.getFileData()) filter in loadWithRetry() by causing the DB to return a file_data row with empty bytes (e.g., a FILEUPDATE with zero-length content), which is silently skipped and falls back to genesis ThrottleDefinitions, allowing gossip of transactions that the current throttle definitions would block?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that cache.get(cacheKey, File.class) returns null when the cache is null (line 122-124) to bypass the cache entirely and always hit the DB, enabling a cache-bypass attack that forces the system to re-evaluate ThrottleDefinitions on every request and potentially load a version that does not enforce gossip throttling?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract-call request with a crafted consensusTimestamp that causes load() to retrieve a stale ThrottleDefinitions file from the cache, resulting in throttle limits being evaluated against outdated quotas and allowing the attacker's transactions to bypass per-transaction-type gossip throttling?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: createMockAddressBook()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded mock address book (127.0.0.3:3, nodeId=3) returned by createMockAddressBook() as the genesis NodeAddressBook to cause the EVM to believe only one node exists in the network, preventing gossip of transactions to all other real nodes and effectively silencing multi-node transaction propagation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FileID with a matching fileNum but a different shardNum or realmNum to bypass the genesisNetworkProperties.fileId().equals(fileId) check, causing the system to query the DB for network properties instead of using the hardcoded genesis values, potentially loading a DB-injected network property that disables gossip for certain transaction types?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that hapiPermissionFile is loaded with a null codec (new SystemFile(load(systemEntity.hapiPermissionFile(), Bytes.EMPTY), null)) and Bytes.EMPTY as genesis content, causing the HAPI permission check to always use empty permissions, which may grant or deny gossip of specific transaction types to all users including unprivileged ones?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of concurrent requests with different consensusTimestamp values to load() for the throttleDefinitionFile, causing cache thrashing where each unique timestamp creates a new CacheKey, filling the cache with stale throttle entries and preventing the current throttle definitions from being enforced, allowing gossip of transactions beyond their defined limits?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the retryTemplate only catches InvalidFileException (predicate: e instanceof InvalidFileException) and not other runtime exceptions, causing an unexpected exception type to propagate out of loadWithRetry() uncaught, crashing the file loading pipeline and causing the caller to fall back to a permissive default that does not enforce gossip throttling?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hour-boundary rounding in roundDownToHour() for feeSchedulesFileId to cause the system to use a fee schedule from the previous hour during the first nanosecond of a new hour, where the new fee schedule has higher fees that would throttle certain transaction types, allowing the attacker to gossip transactions at the old (lower) fee rate before the new schedule takes effect?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that addressBookFile101 and addressBookFile102 both fall back to the mock address book (127.0.0.3:3) when no DB data exists, causing the gossip layer to route all transactions to a single non-existent node (127.0.0.3:3), effectively preventing gossip of any transaction to the real network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic cache check-then-put pattern (cache.get() followed by cache.put() without synchronization) in load() to cause a TOCTOU race where two threads simultaneously find a cache miss, both load different ThrottleDefinitions versions from DB, and one overwrites the other in the cache, resulting in inconsistent throttle enforcement across concurrent transaction gossip attempts?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared AtomicLong nanoSeconds state in loadWithRetry() under concurrent execution to cause timestamp interference between two simultaneous retry sequences, where one thread's nanoSeconds.set(fileData.getConsensusTimestamp() - 1) corrupts the timestamp used by another thread, causing the second thread to load an incorrect (older, more permissive) ThrottleDefinitions version and allowing gossip of throttled transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxExpiry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxExpiry() uses Instant.now().getEpochSecond() + maxLifetime at construction time to create a genesis file with an expiry far in the future, causing the system to never consider the genesis ThrottleDefinitions as expired and always serve it as a valid fallback, even when the current throttle definitions should be enforcing stricter gossip limits?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: isSystemFile(FileID)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a FileID with a fileNum that matches a system file but with a different shard/realm combination to cause isSystemFile() to return false (since the map key uses the full FileID including shard/realm), bypassing the system file loading path and causing the caller to load the file through the non-system path without throttle/fee validation, allowing gossip of transactions that should be blocked?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a future consensusTimestamp (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE) to load(), causing getFileAtTimestamp() to return the most recent file_data row regardless of actual consensus, potentially loading a ThrottleDefinitions version that was submitted but not yet finalized, which may have looser throttle limits that allow gossip of otherwise-blocked transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadWithRetry(FileID, long, SystemFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that codec.parse() is called only for validation (result is discarded) and the actual File is built from the raw bytes regardless, causing a scenario where a partially-valid protobuf that passes codec.parse() but contains malformed ThrottleDefinitions fields is accepted and cached, leading to incorrect throttle enforcement that allows gossip of transactions beyond their defined limits?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the exchangeRatesCacheManager uses a different cache TTL than the defaultSystemFileCacheManager to cause the ThrottleDefinitions (which uses defaultSystemFileCacheManager) to be evicted from cache while the exchange rate file is still cached, creating a window where throttle definitions are re-loaded from DB with a potentially different version that does not enforce gossip throttling for certain transaction types?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: loadAll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that genesisNetworkProperties is loaded with a null codec (new SystemFile(genesisNetworkProperties, null)) to bypass protobuf validation of network properties, allowing a malformed network property file in the DB to be loaded without parse validation, potentially setting a network property that disables gossip throttling for all transaction types?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/state/SystemFileLoader.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: load(FileID, long)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the CacheKey record (FileID + timestamp) to perform a cache key collision attack by finding two different (fileId, consensusTimestamp) pairs that produce the same CacheKey.toString() representation, causing the cache to return a ThrottleDefinitions file for the wrong timestamp, potentially serving a permissive throttle definition and allowing gossip of transactions that should be blocked?
